competitors.name: Supermetrics
strengths: Deep Google properties integration (Ads, Analytics, Looker Studio)
weaknesses: Pricing scales poorly for SMB agencies
Setup time: 4-6 weeks for multi-platform setup
competitors.top_complaint: Price doesn't match value for small agencies
competitors.customer_sentiment: Positive for large agencies; frustrated small agencies switching away

competitors.name: Whatagraph

pricing.beachhead_viability: ⚠️ Affordable but at upper end of SMB budget

strengths: Wider platform support (Ads, Analytics, Facebook, Instagram, LinkedIn, TikTok)
weaknesses: Data refresh lag (6-24 hours, not real-time)
Customer support issues (slow response times reported on Reddit)
competitors.top_complaint: Data freshness issues; clients expect real-time dashboards
competitors.top_praise: White-labeling saves time; competitive advantage with clients
competitors.customer_sentiment: Positive for white-label use case; frustrated with data lag and support

competitors.name: Looker Studio (Google Data Studio)
strengths: Free tier with decent feature set
weaknesses: ZERO automation: Reports must be manually refreshed/regenerated
Not agency-focused; designed for in-house analytics
Scaling to 20+ client reports requires manual setup for each
Requires technical knowledge for complex dashboards
competitors.top_complaint: No automation; requires manual refresh and reporting
competitors.top_praise: Free, beautiful dashboards, excellent for quick BI
competitors.customer_sentiment: Used as foundation but wrapped with manual automation (Zapier, scripts)

competitors.name: Looker (Google Enterprise BI)
competitors.market_position: Enterprise BI platform with marketing connectors

pricing.beachhead_viability: ❌ Out of reach for beachhead segment

weaknesses: Requires SQL and BI team to maintain
competitors.top_complaint: Overly complex for non-technical teams; requires dedicated resources
competitors.top_praise: Powerful and flexible once configured; beautiful dashboards
competitors.customer_sentiment: Excellent for large agencies with BI teams; overkill for SMB

native_tools.name: Facebook Ads Manager Native Reporting
native_tools.pain_point: Siloed (Ads data only; no ROI, leads, or CRM outcomes)

native_tools.name: HubSpot Native Reporting
native_tools.pain_point: CRM-only data; doesn't include paid channel data or attribution

market_pain_points.title: Severe Time Drain on Manual Reporting
evidence: Reddit (r/marketing, r/agencies): '6-10 hours/week manually assembling client reports'
Common workflow: Extract data → create spreadsheets → copy into PowerPoint/PDF
Repeated weekly across 5-30+ clients per agency

market_pain_points.title: Integration Complexity & Data Silos
evidence: G2 reviews: 'Integrations drop data; had to fall back to manual exports'
Reddit: 'No single tool connects all our data sources; we choose primary and manually reconcile'
Competitor weakness: When APIs fail, reports break and require manual intervention

market_pain_points.title: Pricing Misalignment for Small-Mid Agencies
evidence: Reddit: 'We'd love a reporting tool, but $500/month is 10% of what we charge clients. Unsellable.'

market_pain_points.title: Client-Facing Dashboard Limitations
evidence: G2 reviews: 'Dashboard builder is functional but not client-ready; gray, technical'
Reddit: 'Clients expect real-time dashboards but Whatagraph refreshes every 6-24 hours'
Competitor gap: No easy white-labeling or branding for client portals
market_pain_points.unmet_need: Beautiful, embeddable, real-time, white-labeled dashboards clients can consume without training

market_pain_points.title: Agency-Specific Workflow Gap
evidence: Competitors designed for corporate analytics teams (data engineers, analysts, BI specialists)
Assume users know SQL, pivot tables, metric definitions
Don't understand agency workflows: client metrics differ by contract, custom KPIs, multiple campaigns per client
Setup process: 2-6 weeks; agencies need to report next week
market_pain_points.unmet_need: Templated workflows that adapt to common client profiles and automate metric selection

unmet_needs.reportease_solution: Pre-built templates: e-commerce, B2B SaaS, service, local business (1-click setup)

unmet_needs.gap: Clients see stale data; portal UX poor
unmet_needs.reportease_solution: Real-time sync + iframe embedding + white-label branding
Wizard-based setup with smart defaults (30 min → live)

unmet_needs.current_solution: Scale horizontally (N clients = N manual setups)
unmet_needs.reportease_solution: Template inheritance + variable injection for multi-client automation

unmet_needs.gap: Clients don't understand; agencies must explain
unmet_needs.reportease_solution: Pre-built narrative layer: 'You got 150 leads this month, up 20% YoY, at a cost of $12/lead'

reportease_positioning.statement: ReportEase: Automated Marketing Reporting Built for Digital Agencies
reportease_positioning.tagline: 6 hours of weekly reporting → 30 minutes of setup, then automation
reportease_positioning.core_thesis: Purpose-built for the specific workflows, economics, and client management needs of 10-50 person US digital marketing agencies

messaging_hierarchy.message: Automate 6 hours of weekly reporting. ReportEase does in 30 minutes what used to take 30 hours.
Client dashboards that tell a story, not just numbers. White-labeled, real-time, embeddable.
Built for agencies, by people who've done this work. Pricing that makes sense for your budget.

competitive_moat.explanation: Competitors architected around 'flexibility through configuration'; agency needs are afterthought. Rebuilding requires 2-3 years and $3-5M effort.
competitive_moat.why_hard_to_copy: Requires deep agency domain knowledge and platform redesign

competitive_moat.explanation: Competitors can't cut prices without cannibalizing installed base. ReportEase undercuts on price and outcompetes on speed.
competitive_moat.why_hard_to_copy: Locked into higher cost structures; structural disadvantage

competitive_moat.explanation: Achieved through pre-built templates, smart defaults, and agency workflows baked into platform architecture
competitive_moat.why_hard_to_copy: Requires product design decisions embedded in core platform; can't be outsourced

competitive_moat.explanation: Once agencies integrate ReportEase into 10-20 client dashboards, switching costs are high (recreate dashboards, inform clients, re-integrate)
competitive_moat.why_hard_to_copy: Must build same lock-in by starting in beachhead market; incumbent advantage

competitive_moat.explanation: Strong community of agency users becomes 'standard' tool for agencies; word-of-mouth and advocacy are self-reinforcing
competitive_moat.why_hard_to_copy: Years of community building required; can't buy overnight
